samples.gvd:
module pe (
    input clk,
    input rst,
    input [31:0] a,
    input [31:0] b,
    output reg [31:0] c
);

always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
    
    if (rst) begin
        
        c <= 32'b0;
    end else begin
        
        c <= c + (a * b);
    end
end
endmodule

<module id="width_8to16">
    <description>Data width conversion circuit that converts 8-bit data input to 16-bit data output.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating the validity of the input data.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data to be converted.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating the validity of the output data.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output data resulting from the width conversion.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n). On reset, indicated by !rst_n, the data_out register is cleared to 0. If the input data is valid (valid_in) and the flag signal is active, the data_out register is updated by concatenating the contents of the data_lock register (8 bits) and the data_in register (8 bits) to form a 16-bit output. The first valid data is temporarily stored, and when the second valid data is inputted, they are concatenated to produce the output valid_out and data_out.</implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_8bit">
    <description>Multiplies two 8-bit operands using a shift-and-add approach.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[7:0]">First 8-bit input operand (multiplicand).</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[7:0]">Second 8-bit input operand (multiplier).</input>
        <output id="product" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output representing the product of A and B.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Initialize variables
        integer i;
        reg [15:0] product_reg = 16'd0; // Register to hold the product
        reg [7:0] multiplicand = 8'd0;   // Register to hold the multiplicand
        reg [7:0] multiplier = 8'd0;     // Register to hold the multiplier
        
        // Assign input values to local variables
        always @(posedge clk) begin
            multiplicand <= A;
            multiplier <= B;
        end
        
        // Perform multiplication operation
        always @(posedge clk) begin
            if (i < 8) begin
                if (multiplier[i]) begin
                    product_reg <= product_reg + (multiplicand << i);
                end
                i <= i + 1;
            end else begin
                i <= 0;
            end
        end
        
        // Output the final product
        assign product = product_reg;
        
    </implementation>
    
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Parallel to serial converter, where every four input bits are converted to a serial one bit output (from MSB to LSB).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial output representing the converted data.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Assigns the most significant bit of the parallel input to the serial output (dout). On each clock cycle, if the counter (cnt) is 3, indicating the last bit of the parallel input, the module updates the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), resets the counter (cnt) to 0, and sets the valid signal (valid) to 1. Otherwise, the module increments the counter (cnt) by 1, sets the valid signal (valid) to 0, and shifts the data register (data) one bit to the left, with the most significant bit shifted to the least significant bit. Counter Register: If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the register (cnt) is incremented by 1.</implementation>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign id="dout" value="data[3]" />
        <assign id="valid_out" value="valid" />
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <always_comb id="data">
            <if condition="cnt == 3">
                <assign id="data" value="d" />
            </else>
            <else>
                <assign id="data" value="{data[2:0], data[3]}" />
            </else>
        </always_comb>
        <always_comb id="cnt">
            <if condition="!rst_n">
                <assign id="cnt" value="cnt + 1" />
            </else>
        </always_comb>
        <always_comb id="valid">
            <if condition="cnt == 3">
                <assign id="valid" value="1" />
            </else>
            <else>
                <assign id="valid" value="0" />
            </else>
        </always_comb>
    </logic_description>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Serial-to-Parallel Conversion Circuit</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data.</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data (8 bits wide).</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of clk
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Asynchronous reset
                cnt <= 4'd0;
                dout_parallel <= 8'd0;
                dout_valid <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                // Check if din_valid is asserted
                if (din_valid) begin
                    // Shift the incoming serial data into dout_parallel
                    dout_parallel[cnt] <= din_serial;
                    
                    // Increment the counter
                    cnt <= cnt + 1;
                    
                    // Check if 8 bits have been received
                    if (cnt == 4'd7) begin
                        // Set dout_valid to indicate valid output data
                        dout_valid <= 1'b1;
                        
                        // Reset the counter after processing 8 bits
                        cnt <= 4'd0;
                    end else begin
                        // Set dout_valid to indicate invalid output data
                        dout_valid <= 1'b0;
                    end
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Edge detection module for detecting rising and falling edges in a 1-bit input signal 'a'.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Asynchronous reset
                rise <= 1'b0;
                down <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                // Edge detection logic
                if (a && !prev_a) begin
                    // Rising edge detected
                    rise <= 1'b1;
                    down <= 1'b0;
                end else if (!a && prev_a) begin
                    // Falling edge detected
                    rise <= 1'b0;
                    down <= 1'b1;
                end else begin
                    // No edge detected
                    rise <= 1'b0;
                    down <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Register to store previous value of 'a'
        reg prev_a;
        
        // Always block triggered on the positive edge of the clock
        always @(posedge clk) begin
            // Update previous value of 'a'
            prev_a <= a;
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="counter_12">
    <description>Counter from 4 'b0000 to 4' d11. The counting can be controlled by the input signal valid_count. That is, the count is paused if valid_count is 0.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low)</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal</input>
        <input id="valid_count">Signal to enable counting</input>
        <output id="out" width_description="[3:0]">Current count</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // If reset is active, set count to 0
        if (~rst_n) begin
            out <= 4'b0000;
        end else if (valid_count == 1) begin
            // If count is maximum, wrap around to 0
            if (out == 4'd11) begin
                out <= 4'b0000;
            end else begin
                // Otherwise, increment count
                out <= out + 1;
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

module fsm #(
    parameter s0 = 3'b000,
    parameter s1 = 3'b001,
    parameter s2 = 3'b010,
    parameter s3 = 3'b011,
    parameter s4 = 3'b100,
    parameter s5 = 3'b101
) (
    input IN,
    output reg MATCH,
    input CLK,
    input RST
);

    reg [2:0] state, next_state;

    always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
        if (RST) begin
            state <= s0;
        end
        else begin
            state <= next_state;
        end
    end

    always @(*) begin
        case (state)
            s0: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state = s1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state = s0;
                end
            end
            s1: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state = s1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state = s2;
                end
            end
            s2: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state = s1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state = s3;
                end
            end
            s3: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state = s4;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state = s0;
                end
            end
            s4: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state = s5;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state = s0;
                end
            end
            s5: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state = s1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state = s2;
                end
            end
            default: begin
                next_state = s0;
            end
        endcase
    end

    always @(*) begin
        if (RST) begin
            MATCH = 1'b0;
        end
        else if (state == s4 && IN) begin
            MATCH = 1'b1;
        end
        else begin
            MATCH = 1'b0;
        end
    end

endmodule

<module id="fsm">
    <description>Mealy FSM detection circuit that detects a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, output MATCH is 1, and MATCH is 0 in other cases. Supports continuous input and loop detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to the FSM.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="S0">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="S1">State after detecting '1'.</state>
        <state id="S2">State after detecting '10'.</state>
        <state id="S3">State after detecting '100'.</state>
        <state id="S4">State after detecting '1001'.</state>
        <state id="S5">State after detecting '10011'.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="current_state" width_description="[2:0]">Register to hold the current state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" width_description="[2:0]">Register to hold the next state of the FSM.</reg>
        
        // State transition logic
        always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
            if (RST) begin
                current_state <= S0;
            end else begin
                current_state <= next_state;
            end
        end
        
        // Next state logic
        always @(*) begin
            case(current_state)
                S0: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S1;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                    end
                end
                
                S1: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S2;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S1;
                    end
                end
                
                S2: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S3;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S1;
                    end
                end
                
                S3: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S4;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                    end
                end
                
                S4: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S5;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                    end
                end
                
                S5: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S2;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S1;
                    end
                end
                
                default: next_state = S0;
            endcase
        end
        
        // Output logic
        assign MATCH = (current_state == S5 && IN == 1'b1);
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>
        // Sequential logic block triggered on changes in IN, CLK, or RST
        always @(IN or CLK or RST) begin
            // Check for reset condition
            if(RST) begin
                MATCH <= 1'b0; // Set MATCH to 0 on reset
            end else begin
                // State machine logic
                case(current_state)
                    S0: begin
                        if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                            next_state <= S1; // Transition to S1 on IN=1
                        end else begin
                            next_state <= S0; // Stay in S0 on IN=0
                        end
                    end
                    
                    S1: begin
                        if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                            next_state <= S2; // Transition to S2 on IN=0
                        end else begin
                            next_state <= S1; // Stay in S1 on IN=1
                        end
                    end
                    
                    S2: begin
                        if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                            next_state <= S3; // Transition to S3 on IN=0
                        end else begin
                            next_state <= S1; // Stay in S1 on IN=1
                        end
                    end
                    
                    S3: begin
                        if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                            next_state <= S4; // Transition to S4 on IN=1
                        end else begin
                            next_state <= S0; // Go back to S0 on IN=0
                        end
                    end
                    
                    S4: begin
                        if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                            next_state <= S5; // Transition to S5 on IN=1
                        end else begin
                            next_state <= S0; // Go back to S0 on IN=0
                        end
                    end
                    
                    S5: begin
                        if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                            next_state <= S2; // Loop back to S2 on IN=0
                        end else begin
                            next_state <= S1; // Transition to S1 on IN=1
                        end
                    end
                    
                    default: next_state <= S0; // Default to S0
                endcase
                
                // Update MATCH based on current state
                if(next_state == S5 && IN == 1'b1) begin
                    MATCH <= 1'b1; // Set MATCH to 1 if in S5 and IN=1
                end else begin
                    MATCH <= 1'b0; // Otherwise, set MATCH to 0
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="signal_generator">
    <description>Triangle Wave Signal Generator</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <output id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">Generated waveform signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="0">Initial state where the waveform increments.</state>
        <state id="1">State where the waveform decrements.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" width_description="[1:0]">Register to store the current state.</reg>
        <reg id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">Register to store the generated waveform.</reg>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition: set state to 0 and wave to 0
                state <= 0;
                wave <= 0;
            end else begin
                // State machine to generate the triangle waveform
                case(state)
                    0: begin
                        // Increment the waveform value
                        wave <= wave + 1;
                        
                        // Check if the waveform has reached its maximum value
                        if(wave == 31) begin
                            // Transition to state 1 if max value is reached
                            state <= 1;
                        end
                    end
                    
                    1: begin
                        // Decrement the waveform value
                        wave <= wave - 1;
                        
                        // Check if the waveform has reached its minimum value
                        if(wave == 0) begin
                            // Transition back to state 0 if min value is reached
                            state <= 0;
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>Generates a triangular waveform by incrementing and decrementing the wave signal.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_16bit">
    <description>Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Chip clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="start">Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="ain" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand (a) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <input id="bin" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier (b) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <output id="yout" width_description="[31:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 32 bits.</output>
        <output id="done">Chip output flag signal. Defined as 1 indicates the completion of the multiplication operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Reset the shift count register (i) to 0 when the reset signal (rst_n) is low. When the start signal is active (start) and the shift count register (i) is less than 17, increment the shift count register (i) by 1. Reset the shift count register (i) to 0 when the start signal is inactive (!start).</implementation>
    <implementation>Reset the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0 when the reset signal (rst_n) is low. Set the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 1 when the shift count register (i) is equal to 16, indicating the completion of the multiplication operation. Reset the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0 when the shift count register (i) is equal to 17.</implementation>
    <implementation>Reset the multiplicand register (areg), multiplier register (breg), and product register (yout_r) to 0 when the reset signal (rst_n) is low. Start the multiplication operation when the start signal is active (start). Store the multiplicand (ain) and multiplier (bin) in their respective registers (areg and breg) when the shift count register (i) is 0. For shift counts greater than 0 and less than 17, if the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register (areg) is high, accumulate the product register (yout_r) by adding the shifted value of the multiplier register (breg) by shifting it left by i-1 positions and appending zeros at the least significant bit positions.</implementation>
    <implementation>The product output (yout) is assigned the value of the product register (yout_r).</implementation>
    
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="i">Shift count register.</state>
        <state id="done_r">Multiplication completion flag.</state>

        <constant id="one">1.</constant>

<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulates 4 input data values and outputs the result after 4 cycles.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of clk or falling edge of rst_n
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset condition: Set all registers to zero
                data_out <= 10'd0;
                valid_out <= 1'b0;
                counter <= 4'd0;
                temp_sum <= 10'd0;
            end else begin
                // Check if valid_in is asserted
                if (valid_in == 1'b1) begin
                    // Add current data_in value to temp_sum
                    temp_sum <= temp_sum + {2'b00, data_in};
                    
                    // Increment the counter
                    counter <= counter + 1;
                    // Check if we have received 4 input data values
                    if (counter == 4'd4) begin
                        // Update data_out with the accumulated sum
                        data_out <= temp_sum;
                        // Assert valid_out for one cycle
                        valid_out <= 1'b1;
                        // Reset the counter and temp_sum for the next accumulation
                        counter <= 4'd0;
                        temp_sum <= 10'd0;
                    end else begin
                        // Deassert valid_out if not 4 input data values yet
                        valid_out <= 1'b0;
                    end
                end else begin
                    // Deassert valid_out if valid_in is not asserted
                    valid_out <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="adder_pipe_64bit">
    <description>A 64-bit ripple carry adder with pipelining stages.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="i_en">Enable signal for addition operation.</input>
        <input id="adda" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="addb" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="result" width_description="[64:0]">65-bit output representing the sum of adda and addb.</output>
        <output id="o_en">Output enable signal indicating the availability of the result.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Registers for enabling pipeline stages
        reg [63:0] adda_reg;
        reg [63:0] addb_reg;
        reg [64:0] sum_reg_stage1;
        reg [64:0] sum_reg_stage2;
        reg [64:0] sum_reg_stage3;
        reg [64:0] sum_reg_stage4;
        
        // Register for storing the final result
        reg [64:0] result_reg;
        
        // Register for synchronizing the input enable signal
        reg i_en_sync1;
        reg i_en_sync2;
        
        // Register for storing the output enable signal
        reg o_en_reg;
        
        // Assigning the output enable signal
        assign o_en = o_en_reg;
        
        // Assigning the result register to the output port
        assign result = result_reg;
        
        // Sequential logic for updating the pipeline stages
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (~rst_n) begin
                // Reset all pipeline stages and registers
                adda_reg <= 64'd0;
                addb_reg <= 64'd0;
                sum_reg_stage1 <= 65'd0;
                sum_reg_stage2 <= 65'd0;
                sum_reg_stage3 <= 65'd0;
                sum_reg_stage4 <= 65'd0;
                result_reg <= 65'd0;
                i_en_sync1 <= 1'b0;
                i_en_sync2 <= 1'b0;
                o_en_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                // Synchronize the input enable signal through two flip-flops
                i_en_sync1 <= i_en;
                i_en_sync2 <= i_en_sync1;
                
                // Update the pipeline stages based on the input enable signal
                if (i_en_sync2) begin
                    adda_reg <= adda;
                    addb_reg <= addb;
                    
                    // Calculate the sum for each pipeline stage
                    sum_reg_stage1 <= {1'b0, adda} + {1'b0, addb};
                    sum_reg_stage2 <= sum_reg_stage1 + {1'b0, sum_reg_stage1[64]};
                    sum_reg_stage3 <= sum_reg_stage2 + {1'b0, sum_reg_stage2[64]};
                    sum_reg_stage4 <= sum_reg_stage3 + {1'b0, sum_reg_stage3[64]};
                    
                    // Store the final result in the result register
                    result_reg <= sum_reg_stage4;
                    
                    // Set the output enable signal to indicate the availability of the result
                    o_en_reg <= 1'b1;
                end else begin
                    // Clear the output enable signal if the input enable signal is not asserted
                    o_en_reg <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

module JC_counter (
    input                clk,
    input                rst_n,
    output reg [63:0]     Q
);

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    
    if (~rst_n) begin
        Q <= 64'b0;
    end else begin
        
        if (Q[0] == 1'b0) begin
            Q <= {1'b1, Q[63:1]};
        end else begin
            
            Q <= {1'b0, Q[63:1]};
        end
    end
end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="JC_counter">
    <description>64-bit Johnson Counter</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal to initialize the counter.</input>
        <output id="Q" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit register representing the current count value.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>On every rising edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the counter value is updated. If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the counter value (Q) is set to 0. Otherwise, if the least significant bit (Q[0]) is 0, the counter increments by shifting the current value (Q[63:1]) to the right and appending a 1 at the most significant bit position. If the least significant bit (Q[0]) is 1, the counter decrements by shifting the current value (Q[63:1]) to the right and appending a 0 at the most significant bit position.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="right_shifter">
    <description>Right shifter module that performs an 8-bit right shift on a 1-bit input.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="d">Input signal to be right-shifted.</input>
        <output id="q">Output signal representing the result of the right shift operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Initialize q register to 0. On each rising edge of the clock, shift the contents of q to the right by one bit and insert the new input bit d into the most significant position of the register.</implementation>
    
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="q" width_description="[7:0]">Register to store the shifted values.</reg>
        
        <initial>
            <assign value="0">Initial value of q is set to 0.</assign>
        </initial>
        
        <always @("posedge clk")>
            <assign value="(q >> 1)">Shift the contents of q to the right by one bit.</assign>
            <assign value="d">Insert the new input bit d into the most significant position of the register.</assign>
        </always>
    </logic_description>
</module>

module RAM #(
    parameter WIDTH=6,
    parameter DEPTH=8
) (
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input write_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]write_addr,
    input [WIDTH-1:0]write_data,
    input read_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]read_addr,
    output reg [WIDTH-1:0]read_data
);

reg [7 - 1 : 0] RAM [2**6-1:0];

integer i;
initial begin
    for(i = 0; i < 2**6; i = i + 1) begin
        RAM[i] = 6'b000000;
    end
end

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        
        for(i = 0; i < 2**6; i = i + 1) begin
            RAM[i] <= 6'b000000;
        end
    end else if(write_en == 1) begin
        
        RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
    end
end

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        
        read_data <= 6'b000000;
    end else if(read_en == 1) begin
        
        read_data <= RAM[read_addr];
    end else begin
        
        read_data <= 6'b000000;
    end
end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="RAM">
    <description>A dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6, where all data is initialized to 000000. It supports simultaneous read and write operations. When read_en is 1, the read_data at the specified read_addr is read. When write_en is 1, data is written to the RAM at the specified write_addr.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="write_en">Write enable signal to initiate a write operation.</input>
        <input id="write_addr" width_description="[5:0]">Address for the write operation.</input>
        <input id="write_data" width_description="[5:0]">Data to be written to the RAM.</input>
        <input id="read_en">Read enable signal to initiate a read operation.</input>
        <input id="read_addr" width_description="[5:0]">Address for the read operation.</input>
        <output id="read_data" width_description="[5:0]">Output signal representing the data read from the RAM.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="WIDTH" value="6">Bit width parameter for the RAM.</constant>
        <constant id="DEPTH" value="8">Depth parameter for the RAM.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <reg id="read_data" width_description="[5:0]">Register to hold the read data.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Define the RAM array
        reg [DEPTH - 1 : 0] RAM [2**WIDTH-1:0];
        
        // Initialize the RAM contents to 0
        integer i;
        initial begin
            for(i = 0; i < 2**WIDTH; i = i + 1) begin
                RAM[i] = 6'b000000;
            end
        end
        
        // Write operation
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset the RAM contents to 0
                for(i = 0; i < 2**WIDTH; i = i + 1) begin
                    RAM[i] <= 6'b000000;
                end
            end else if(write_en == 1) begin
                // Write data to the RAM at the specified address
                RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
            end
        end
        
        // Read operation
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset the read data to 0
                read_data <= 6'b000000;
            end else if(read_en == 1) begin
                // Read data from the RAM at the specified address
                read_data <= RAM[read_addr];
            end else begin
                // Clear the read data if read_en is not active
                read_data <= 6'b000000;
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="synchronizer">
    <description>A multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer. Data remains constant during the period when data_en is high, and data_en is high for at least 3 clk_b clock cycles. When the value of data_en is high, data can be synchronized. The data change frequency of data_in is very low. The change interval between two adjacent data is at least 10 clk_b clock cycles.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="arstn">Active-low reset signal A. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="brstn">Active-low reset signal B. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data signal of size 4 bits.</input>
        <input id="data_en">Input enable signal that controls the selection operation.</input>
        <output id="dataout" width_description="[3:0]">Output data signal of size 4 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Data Register
        reg [3:0] data_reg;
        always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
            if (!arstn) begin
                data_reg <= 4'b0;
            end else begin
                data_reg <= data_in;
            end
        end
        
        // Enable Data Register
        reg en_data_reg;
        always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
            if (!arstn) begin
                en_data_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                en_data_reg <= data_en;
            end
        end
        
        // Enable Control Registers
        reg en_clap_one;
        reg en_clap_two;
        
        always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
            if (!brstn) begin
                en_clap_one <= 1'b0;
                en_clap_two <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                en_clap_one <= en_data_reg;
                en_clap_two <= en_clap_one;
            end
        end
        
        // Output Assignment
        reg [3:0] dataout;
        always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
            if (!brstn) begin
                dataout <= 4'b0;
            end else begin
                if (en_clap_two) begin
                    dataout <= data_reg;
                end else begin
                    dataout <= dataout;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="freq_div">
    <description>Frequency Divider for generating different clock frequencies based on input clock signal.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK_in">Input clock signal.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal.</input>
        <output id="CLK_50">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2.</output>
        <output id="CLK_10">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10.</output>
        <output id="CLK_1">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Three counters are used to generate different clock frequencies based on input clock signal.</implementation>
    
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="cnt_10" width_description="[3:0]">Counter for generating CLK_10.</reg>
        <reg id="cnt_100" width_description="[6:0]">Counter for generating CLK_1.</reg>
        
        <always @(posedge CLK_in or posedge RST)> 
            if(RST) begin
                cnt_10 <= 4'd0;
                cnt_100 <= 7'd0;
            end else begin
                if(cnt_10 == 4'd4) begin
                    cnt_10 <= 4'd0;
                end else begin
                    cnt_10 <= cnt_10 + 1;
                end
                
                if(cnt_100 == 7'd49) begin
                    cnt_100 <= 7'd0;
                end else begin
                    cnt_100 <= cnt_100 + 1;
                end
            end
        </always>
        
        <assign CLK_50 = ~CLK_50;
        <assign CLK_10 = (cnt_10 == 4'd4) ? ~CLK_10 : CLK_10;
        <assign CLK_1 = (cnt_100 == 7'd49) ? ~CLK_1 : CLK_1;
    </logic_description>
endmodule

<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 2*size bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="size">Number of bits in the multiplier and multiplicand.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign id="mul_a_ext" value="'{size{1'b0}}, mul_a}"/>
        <assign id="mul_b_ext" value="'{size{1'b0}}, mul_b}"/>
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        genvar i;
        generate
            for(i = 0; i < size; i=i+1) begin : gen_partial_products
                if(i == 0) begin
                    assign id="partial_product[0]" value="(mul_b[i]) ? mul_a_ext : 'd0";
                end else begin
                    assign id="partial_product[{i}] value="(mul_b[i]) ? (mul_a_ext << i) : 'd0";
                end
            end
        endgenerate
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        reg [size*2-1:0] regs[size-1:0];
        integer j;
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                for(j = 0; j < size; j=j+1) begin
                    regs[j] <= {size*2{1'b0}};
                end
            end else begin
                for(j = 0; j < size; j=j+1) begin
                    regs[j] <= regs[j] + partial_product[j];
                end
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        reg [size*2-1:0] mul_out_reg;
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                mul_out_reg <= {size*2{1'b0}};
            end else begin
                mul_out_reg <= regs[size-1];
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Extends inputs by adding zeros at the MSB positions. Performs multiplication using a generate block to calculate partial products based on the multiplier's bits. Registers are used to store intermediate sums, and the final product is calculated by adding the last register value to the product output register.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse detection module for detecting rising edges in data_in.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_START">State indicating the start of a pulse.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_END">State indicating the end of a pulse.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" width_description="[2:0]">Register to store the current state.</reg>
        <wire id="next_state">Next state for the state machine.</wire>
        <always @(*) begin
            case(state)
                IDLE: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1 && ~data_in_prev) begin
                        next_state = PULSE_START;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = IDLE;
                    end
                end
                PULSE_START: begin
                    if(~data_in) begin
                        next_state = PULSE_END;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = PULSE_START;
                    end
                end
                PULSE_END: begin
                    if(data_in) begin
                        next_state = IDLE;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = PULSE_END;
                    end
                end
                default: next_state = IDLE;
            endcase
        end
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(~rst_n) begin
                state <= IDLE;
            end else begin
                state <= next_state;
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        Always block triggered on the positive edge of the clk signal and the negative edge of the rst_n signal.
        
        In the reset condition (~rst_n), initialize the state register to the IDLE state and set the data_out output to 0, indicating no pulse detected.
        
        For each state, determine the next state based on the current state and the values of data_in and data_in_prev.
        
        Update the state register with the next state during the positive edge of the clk signal.
        
        Set data_out to 1 if the current state is PULSE_END, indicating the end of a pulse, otherwise set it to 0.
    </implementation>
    
    <task id="reset_task">
        <description>Task to reset the state register and data_out output.</description>
        <step>Set state register to IDLE state.</step>
        <step>Set data_out output to 0.</step>
    </task>
    
    <task id="update_state_task">
        <description>Task to update the state register based on the current state and inputs.</description>
        <step>Check the current state and inputs to determine the next state.</step>
        <step>Update the state register with the next state.</step>
    </task>
    
    <task id="set_data_out_task">
        <description>Task to set the data_out output based on the current state.</description>
        <step>Check the current state to determine the value of data_out.</step>
        <step>Set data_out output accordingly.</step>
    </task>
    
    <task id="main_task">
        <description>Main task to control the operation of the pulse detect module.</description>
        <step>Trigger the reset_task when rst_n is low.</step>
        <step>Trigger the update_state_task on the positive edge of clk.</step>
        <step>Trigger the set_data_out_task on the positive edge of clk.</step>
    </main_task>
endmodule

<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>A pipelined unsigned 8-bit multiplier.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal controlling the multiplication process.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplicand input.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Multiplier input.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating when the multiplication result is valid.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Implements a pipelined unsigned 8-bit multiplier using a combination of input control, input registers, partial product generation, partial sum calculation, final product calculation, and output assignment stages.</implementation>
    <task id="assign_output">
        <description>Assigns the output based on the output enable signal and the product register value.</description>
        <implementation>if (mul_en_out) begin
            mul_out = mul_out_reg;
        end else begin
            mul_out = 0;
        end</implementation>
    </task>
    
    <parameter_description>
        <constant name="WIDTH_A" type="numeric" value="8"/> <!-- Width of the multiplicand -->
        <constant name="WIDTH_B" type="numeric" value="8"/> <!-- Width of the multiplier -->
        <constant name="WIDTH_OUT" type="numeric" value="16"/> <!-- Width of the product output -->
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_en_out_reg" width_description="[1:0]">Register to hold the output enable signal.</reg>
        
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)>
            if (~rst_n) begin
                mul_en_out_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                mul_en_out_reg[1] <= mul_en_out_reg[0];
                mul_en_out_reg[0] <= mul_en_in;
            end
        end
        
        <assign id="mul_en_out" value="{mul_en_out_reg[1]}"/>
        
        <reg id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[WIDTH_A-1:0]">Register to hold the multiplicand.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[WIDTH_B-1:0]">Register to hold the multiplier.</reg>
        
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)>
            if (~rst_n) begin
                mul_a_reg <= 0;
                mul_b_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                mul_a_reg <= mul_a;
                mul_b_reg <= mul_b;
            end
        end
        
        <wire id="temp[0]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Wire to hold the first partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[1]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Wire to hold the second partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[2]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Wire to hold the third partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[3]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Wire to hold the fourth partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[4]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Wire to hold the fifth partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[5]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Wire to hold the sixth partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[6]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Wire to hold the seventh partial product.</wire>
        <wire id="temp[7]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Wire to hold the eighth partial product.</wire>
        
        <assign id="temp[0]" value="{9'd0,mul_a_reg[0]&mul_b_reg}"/>
        <assign id="temp[1]" value="{8'd0,mul_a_reg[1]&mul_b_reg,1'd0}"/>
        <assign id="temp[2]" value="{7'd0,mul_a_reg[2]&mul_b_reg,2'd0}"/>
        <assign id="temp[3]" value="{6'd0,mul_a_reg[3]&mul_b_reg,3'd0}"/>
        <assign id="temp[4]" value="{5'd0,mul_a_reg[4]&mul_b_reg,4'd0}"/>
        <assign id="temp[5]" value="{4'd0,mul_a_reg[5]&mul_b_reg,5'd0}"/>
        <assign id="temp[6]" value="{3'd0,mul_a_reg[6]&mul_b_reg,6'd0}"/>
        <assign id="temp[7]" value="{2'd0,mul_a_reg[7]&mul_b_reg,7'd0}"/>
        
        <reg id="sum[0]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Register to hold the first partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[1]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Register to hold the second partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[2]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Register to hold the third partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[3]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Register to hold the fourth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[4]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Register to hold the fifth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[5]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Register to hold the sixth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[6]" width_description="[WIDTH_A+WIDTH_B-1:0]">Register to hold the seventh partial sum.</reg>
        
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)>
            if (~rst_n) begin
                sum[0] <= 0;
                sum[1] <= 0;
                sum[2] <= 0;
                sum[3] <= 0;
                sum[4] <= 0;
                sum[5] <= 0;
                sum[6] <= 0;
            end else begin
                sum[0] <= temp[0];
                sum[1] <= temp[1] + temp[2];
                sum[2] <= temp[3] + temp[4];
                sum[3] <= temp[5] + temp[6];
                sum[4] <= temp[7];
            end
        end
        
        <reg id="mul_out_reg" width_description="[WIDTH_OUT-1:0]">Register to hold the final product output.</reg>
        
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)>
            if (~rst_n) begin
                mul_out_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                mul_out_reg <= sum[0] + sum[1] + sum[2] + sum[3] + sum[4];
            end
        end
        
        <assign id="mul_out" value="mul_out_reg"/>
    </logic_description>
endmodule

<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>A traffic light system with red, yellow, and green indicators, and a pedestrian button.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Request signal for allowing vehicles to pass.</input>
        <output id="clock" width_description="[7:0]">An 8-bit output representing the count value of the internal counter.</output>
        <output id="red">Output signal representing the state of the red indicator.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Output signal representing the state of the yellow indicator.</output>
        <output id="green">Output signal representing the state of the green indicator.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="idle">Initial state where all signals are set to 0.</state>
        <state id="s1_red">State where the red signal is set to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s3_green state.</state>
        <state id="s2_yellow">State where the yellow signal is set to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s1_red state.</state>
        <state id="s3_green">State where the green signal is set to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s2_yellow state.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="cnt" width_description="[7:0]">Internal counter for timing control.</reg>
        <reg id="state" width_description="[1:0]">Register to hold the current state of the traffic light controller.</reg>
        <reg id="p_red">Previous value of the red signal.</reg>
        <reg id="p_yellow">Previous value of the yellow signal.</reg>
        <reg id="p_green">Previous value of the green signal.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // State transition logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                state <= idle;
                p_red <= 0;
                p_yellow <= 0;
                p_green <= 0;
            end else begin
                case(state)
                    idle: begin
                        state <= s1_red;
                        p_red <= 0;
                        p_yellow <= 0;
                        p_green <= 0;
                    end
                    s1_red: begin
                        if(cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                            p_red <= 0;
                            p_yellow <= 0;
                            p_green <= 1;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                            p_red <= 1;
                            p_yellow <= 0;
                            p_green <= 0;
                        end
                    end
                    s2_yellow: begin
                        if(cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                            p_red <= 1;
                            p_yellow <= 0;
                            p_green <= 0;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                            p_red <= 0;
                            p_yellow <= 1;
                            p_green <= 0;
                        end
                    end
                    s3_green: begin
                        if(cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                            p_red <= 0;
                            p_yellow <= 1;
                            p_green <= 0;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                            p_red <= 0;
                            p_yellow <= 0;
                            p_green <= 1;
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        // Counting logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                cnt <= 10;
            end else begin
                if(pass_request && green) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else if(!green && p_green) begin
                    cnt <= 60;
                end else if(!yellow && p_yellow) begin
                    cnt <= 5;
                end else if(!red && p_red) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else begin
                    cnt <= cnt - 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assign statements for outputs
        assign clock = cnt;
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                red <= 0;
                yellow <= 0;
                green <= 0;
            end else begin
                red <= p_red;
                yellow <= p_yellow;
                green <= p_green;
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Asynchronous FIFO implementation with configurable parameters WIDTH and DEPTH.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH" value="8">FIFO data width.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH" value="16">FIFO depth.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Define internal signals
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] waddr;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] raddr;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] wptr;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] rptr;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] wptr_buff;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] rptr_buff;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] waddr_bin;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] raddr_bin;
        reg [$clog2(DEPTH):0] rptr_syn;
        // Convert binary to Gray code
        function [$clog2(DEPTH):0] bin_to_gray(input [$clog2(DEPTH):0] bin);
            begin
                bin_to_gray = bin ^ (bin >> 1);
            end
        endfunction
        // Convert Gray code to binary
        function [$clog2(DEPTH):0] gray_to_bin(input [$clog2(DEPTH):0] gray);
            integer i;
            begin
                gray_to_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)] = gray[$clog2(DEPTH)];
                for(i = $clog2(DEPTH)-1; i >= 0; i=i-1) begin
                    gray_to_bin[i] = gray_to_bin[i+1] ^ gray[i];
                end
            end
        endfunction
        // Update write pointer
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if(~wrstn) begin
                waddr <= 0;
                wptr <= 0;
            end else begin
                if(winc && !wfull) begin
                    waddr <= waddr + 1;
                    wptr <= bin_to_gray(waddr + 1);
                end
            end
        end
        // Update read pointer
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if(~rrstn) begin
                raddr <= 0;
                rptr <= 0;
            end else begin
                if(rinc && !rempty) begin
                    raddr <= raddr + 1;
                    rptr <= bin_to_gray(raddr + 1);
                end
            end
        end
        // Synchronize read pointer to write clock domain
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if(~wrstn) begin
                rptr_syn <= 0;
                rptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                rptr_syn <= rptr;
                rptr_buff <= rptr_syn;
            end
        end
        // Synchronize write pointer to read clock domain
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if(~rrstn) begin
                wptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                wptr_buff <= wptr;
            end
        end
        // Assign output signals
        assign wfull = (wptr == {~rptr_buff[$clog2(DEPTH)], rptr_buff[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]});
        assign rempty = (rptr == wptr_buff);
        assign rdata = raddr_bin;
        // Connect input signals to dual-port RAM module
        assign wen = winc & !wfull;
        assign ren = rinc & !rempty;
        assign wdata = wdata;
        assign rdata = rdata;
        
        // Dual-port RAM instance
        dual_port_RAM #(WIDTH, DEPTH) ram_inst(
            .wclk(wclk),
            .rclk(rclk),
            .wen(wen),
            .ren(ren),
            .waddr(waddr),
            .raddr(raddr),
            .wdata(wdata),
            .rdata(rdata)
        );
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Implements an asynchronous FIFO using dual-port RAM, write and read controllers, and pointer synchronizers. Uses Gray code for read and write pointers. Implements full and empty flags.</implementation>
</module>
